fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Ohio Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer occupational therapist license to ohio, occupational therapist compact ohio, ohio occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Ohio OT, PT, and Athletic Trainers Board
board.mailing_address: 77 S. High Street, 16th Floor, Columbus, Ohio 43215-6108

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Ohio.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Ohio Account
steps.description: Register for a new account on the eLicense Ohio portal if you do not already have one. This is the primary system for all licensing activities.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Log in to eLicense Ohio and complete the online application for Occupational Therapist Licensure by Examination or Endorsement. Pay the required application and initial license fees through the portal.

steps.title: Send Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying your occupational therapy degree, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Ohio Occupational Therapy, Physical Therapy, and Athletic Trainers Board.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Exam Scores
steps.description: Ensure your official National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam scores are sent directly from NBCOT to the Ohio Occupational Therapy, Physical Therapy, and Athletic Trainers Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete a criminal background check, including both BCI (Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation) and FBI (Federal Bureau of Investigation) checks. The results must be sent directly to the Ohio Board.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application on your eLicense Ohio account. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Ohio OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Ohio has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Ohio?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Ohio
hero.dek: Occupational Therapists in Ohio apply for licensure through the eLicense Ohio portal, which handles all new applications, renewals, and supporting document submissions.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the eLicense Ohio portal and click 'Register a New Account'. Follow the prompts to agree to terms, provide and verify your email, create a username and password, and set up security questions. Once registered, you can log in to start your application.
